[2018-04-28 04:14] Deleted User (Deleted User): math proves iso sucks
[2018-04-28 04:14] redrame: I definitely did more than just that but not publically
[2018-04-28 04:14] mrguy888: apooche did that match and i like apooche math
[2018-04-28 04:14] redrame: Pooch math isn’t hard though
[2018-04-28 04:14] mtanzer: when I first did the math for all the units I remember one thing that stood out to me was how close in value most of the random set attackers were.  Like which ones you go for should really depend on ingame factors like tech/economy setup, support, etc. more than raw power level.
[2018-04-28 04:15] redrame: Pooch math gets you yujiri’s model of 5GGB donger
[2018-04-28 04:15] redrame: There seems to be a contradiction here
[2018-04-28 04:15] mtanzer: so if people are doing math trying to rank units relatively or compare units directly to each other and weighting that higher than the factors of the actual game situation then yeah it'd be a problem
[2018-04-28 04:16] mrguy888: A graph is more useful than a number
[2018-04-28 04:16] redrame: Ok, that is very true
[2018-04-28 04:16] mtanzer: yes but a graph is also more of a pain to produce and a number is still pretty useful a lot of the time imo.  I think it's that last part that we disagree on
[2018-04-28 04:16] redrame: People spitting bullshit numbers is a big part of the problem
[2018-04-28 04:17] redrame: Graphs are not too hard
[2018-04-28 04:17] mrguy888: like apooche math has extremes that you can use your player knowledge to find out that it lives on the extremems
[2018-04-28 04:17] redrame: They’re so simple I can do them
[2018-04-28 04:17] mrguy888: while yujiri would give some middling number that doesnt apply
[2018-04-28 04:17] mtanzer: they're simple but doing them for 100 units is tedious
[2018-04-28 04:18] mtanzer: if there are specific units you really care about I can make a graph for them
[2018-04-28 04:18] mrguy888: yeah but that amount of tedium is needed if you want it to be meaningfull
[2018-04-28 04:18] mrguy888: im calling out laziness not stupidity
[2018-04-28 04:18] redrame: I agree with mrguy.  Which is why the project is stupid
[2018-04-28 04:18] redrame: In fact I would say even mrguys approach is insufficient
[2018-04-28 04:19] mrguy888: well if i were to do math i would do a lot more than make graphs
[2018-04-28 04:19] mrguy888: but im not about to learn math for prismata
[2018-04-28 04:19] mtanzer: I guess the math I've done has always been primarily to inform myself and not others.  When others ask me questions about the math I answer them using what I've already done for myself but I've never tried to make anything comprehensive or universally useful?
[2018-04-28 04:20] mtanzer: so like for me to get the information I want about a unit I'm happy to just get a few data points for different R's rather than make a graph, and I feel like I can also answer questions sufficiently with this approach
[2018-04-28 04:20] mrguy888: like i dont think apooches math is some magical work of genius i think it is the minimum needed to be functional
[2018-04-28 04:20] redrame: Data points work.  You need enough points that it’s more work than a graph
[2018-04-28 04:21] mrguy888: a graph is just all the data points
[2018-04-28 04:21] mtanzer: well it depends what info you want
[2018-04-28 04:21] mtanzer: I'm perfectly happy knowing how good units are at R = 1.4 except for a handful of units that depend heavily on R
[2018-04-28 04:21] mrguy888: but if you REALLY want all the data points you need many graphs
[2018-04-28 04:21] mtanzer: I'm aware which units get better/worse as you increase/decrease R
[2018-04-28 04:22] mtanzer: for the extreme units like zemora I look more closely at a bunch of different values if I have to
[2018-04-28 04:22] mtanzer: although tbh with zemora it's probably easier to model it by comparing lines
[2018-04-28 04:22] mrguy888: zemora seems like it would be a less extreme unit
[2018-04-28 04:22] mtanzer: zemora depends a lot on R just because of the large build-time
[2018-04-28 04:23] mrguy888: yeah but you get it so early that a lot of the turns are independant
[2018-04-28 04:23] mrguy888: of the set cause opponent drones up
[2018-04-28 04:24] mtanzer: yeah that's why I mentioned comparing lines being possibly easier
[2018-04-28 04:24] mtanzer: like to take that to the extreme for savior I've never even bothered trying to do math, I much prefer to just open analysis
[2018-04-28 04:24] mtanzer: because the math would be so complicated anyway
[2018-04-28 04:26] mtanzer: speaking of savior...apooche going for #1 in an asymmetric savior game right now!
[2018-04-28 04:30] mrguy888: Should I go for 2200? On one hand that seems to be what all the cool kids are into nowadays but on the other hand I could just not and lose nothing. 🤔
[2018-04-28 04:35] mtanzer: ok two more things I wanted to add before I leave for dinner:
1.  Regarding the old redeemer, I suspect that wall being op and it enabling one player to get wall absorb value a lot earlier than usual is the main problem with the rush and that "in a vacuum" the unit is pretty strong but not way too strong.  I could try to do math for that but tbh I think it'd be a lot more work than just opening up analysis mode since accounting for the specifics of the rush probably comes close to just playing the first few turns out on paper anyway.
2.  Yes my assumptions are usually on the lazy side and maybe whenever math comes up I should emphasize (even more than I already do) how real-game factors are usually what you use to decide turns and strategies a lot moreso than math.  I guess instead of being told "your math is bad/useless" I prefer being told "I don't like assumptions X,Y,Z, can you change it to add/subtract these other assumptions."
[2018-04-28 04:35] mrguy888: Why not is never the question you ask. Inaction has no cost. Action has a cost.
[2018-04-28 04:37] mrguy888: I would do 2 if I could. All I understand is the output. 😕
[2018-04-28 04:38] mtanzer: yes I admit it's also reasonable to want me to make good assumptions to begin with since I'm the one doing the math lol
[2018-04-28 04:38] mtanzer: I try to find a balance between assumptions that give decent information and difficulty to implement

[2018-04-28 05:32] apooche: one issue I've had with showing results using math in mata is that people usually interpret it as if I'm assuming a lot less than I am, even if I carefully state my assumptions. also explaining when certain decisions about assumptions are bad/good
[2018-04-28 05:32] apooche: but really it isn't the results of the math that is important, it is understanding what went into the calculation and figuring out what differences there are in a real board position
[2018-04-28 05:33] apooche: like, the important thing in holding a unit such as omega splitter is how much tempo, absorb, and damage you get. but people see the math and go "oh ok so always hold omega"
[2018-04-28 05:33] mrguy888: Yeah and that is what is missing from other peoples stuff that they put.
[2018-04-28 05:33] apooche: and its like no, hold omega when the tempo and absorb are enough to outweigh the damage, which in the case where everyone is completely granular they do
[2018-04-28 05:34] mrguy888: People post a single number and often say nothing about their assumptions unless prodded.

I know that can be often irrelevant in base+8 but at least I can quite confidently say who is favoured in a vacuum. Calling it easy does not sound far fetched. Probably Yujiri and I spent the most time on this kind of stuff.

messages.author: steel0229e
messages.content: Playing an electrovore mirror is definitely a lot easier than playing a midrangey deck with 4-5 units.

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-05-05) ---
[2018-05-05 09:37] antlion7656: Hi, everyone, I am new to Prismata and I just won a game I thought I was behind whole game.
Anyone mind to review the replay and  analyze what happened ?
Here is the replay : http://play.prismata.net/?r=adByh-U194q
Thanks !
[2018-05-05 09:40] crash_overlord: You were just kinda ahead the whole time cause you bought better units. Grimbotch > tarsiers. Asteri > tarsier. Valkyrion > Tarsier.
[2018-05-05 09:41] antlion7656: I thought Tarsier is always good since they are cheap and doesn't die after 4 turns.
[2018-05-05 09:42] crash_overlord: But they're slower. Attacking a turn earlier so that you can then get 2 defense in 4 turns >>>>>>>>>>>>> Tarsier.
[2018-05-05 09:45] antlion7656: That make sense. Then I wonder should I block or attack with Grimbotch when his lifespan=1?
[2018-05-05 09:45] itsotter: attacking with it spends 2 life to deal 1 damage
[2018-05-05 09:46] itsotter: this is worth it if you're breaching to kill shadowfang of course
[2018-05-05 09:46] itsotter: but in general, the value is gotten by holding grimbotch at 1
[2018-05-05 09:47] itsotter: also tarsier is not bad, if there's no attackers in the set then tarsier is best attacker by default
[2018-05-05 09:47] antlion7656: So grimbotch is like 4 gold+ 1 red for 3 damages and 2 life most of the time?
[2018-05-05 09:47] itsotter: but after you make your first 2, you usually want to find a way to buy a non-delayed attacker so that you have 3 or more damage when those initial tarsiers begin firing
[2018-05-05 09:47] itsotter: otherwise they will get fully absorbed by a simple wall and it sucks
[2018-05-05 09:48] amalloy: @antlion7656 that is how it is generally spent, yes, but it's important that you don't get the 3 damage and 2 health all at once - it would be way too good if it did that. fast stuff is generally better than slow stuff
[2018-05-05 09:52] itsotter: because of exponential economy growth, the very earliest turns are the most important, which explains why tarsier is unexpectedly bad
[2018-05-05 09:52] antlion7656: Seems i need much more time to understand Prismata
[2018-05-05 09:52] itsotter: tarsier can fire infinitely late into the game, but the single most important turn the enemy will take is the one right after yours ends
[2018-05-05 09:53] itsotter: and on that turn, he's not under pressure from the tarsier you made
[2018-05-05 09:53] itsotter: so he gets to make more econ and attack (rather than defend that tarsier)
[2018-05-05 09:53] itsotter: that early advantage wil snowball as the game proceeds just because of how the game works
[2018-05-05 09:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): Basically tarsier is horrid
[2018-05-05 09:54] itsotter: that is more succinct
[2018-05-05 09:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): 4th turn+ should be virtually irrelevant
[2018-05-05 09:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): grimbotch is one of the best units, tarsier is one of the worst
[2018-05-05 09:55] Deleted User (Deleted User): of course u should be sure to get full value out of ur botch
[2018-05-05 09:55] Deleted User (Deleted User): but a full value botch is just always better than a tarsier
[2018-05-05 09:55] antlion7656: I thought Tarsier is good since infinite damage with 4 gold + 1 red  (cheap)
[2018-05-05 09:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): well the value of damage goes down over time
[2018-05-05 09:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): value of everything goes down over time
[2018-05-05 09:56] amalloy: it's "infinite", but most things in prismata are infinite
[2018-05-05 09:56] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): But all basic set units are "bad"
[2018-05-05 09:56] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): It's still a better attacker than Steelsplitter or Gauss Cannon
[2018-05-05 09:57] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): And sometimes Tarsier will be the best attacker in a set if all the random set units are econ or defense-based
[2018-05-05 09:57] itsotter: it is hard to stop overvaluing long-term investments
[2018-05-05 09:57] itsotter: i think all new players just keep making drones every turn
[2018-05-05 09:57] itsotter: at least that is how it was for me
[2018-05-05 09:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): like infinite tarsier damage only matters if there's no attacker supplies
[2018-05-05 09:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): otherwise the fact is, you're paying such a hefty price
[2018-05-05 09:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): 4R bt2 is *expensive*
[2018-05-05 09:58] antlion7656: Yeah, I have no idea how to estimate number of drones I need every different sets
[2018-05-05 09:59] amalloy: there are rules of thumb, but you'll get better with practice
[2018-05-05 09:59] Deleted User (Deleted User): I hear all the time that players(noobs) say tarsier is cheap
[2018-05-05 09:59] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): There's a good guide by 307th here if you haven't read it https://prismatalibrary.blog/2017/12/22/the-standard-style/
The Standard Style
This guide will teach you the standard style of play – a style of play that is simple enough for a beginner to be able to learn, while still being grounded in real high level Prismata strateg…
[2018-05-05 10:00] vanitascabal6962 (VanitasCabal): It goes into some of the basics that seem overwhelming at first
[2018-05-05 10:00] Deleted User (Deleted User): I don't know which mistake they're making. is it 

1) they don't factor bt in the cost
2) they think 4R bt2 is cheap?
[2018-05-05 10:00] itsotter: 307th guide will tell you basically that you can rule-of-thumb your target drone count by the size of the absorber you're getting
[2018-05-05 10:00] itsotter: not a perfect rule because other things can dictate the pace besides absorber size
[2018-05-05 10:01] itsotter: but good for starting out
[2018-05-05 10:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): well I'll address both. 

1) you have to factor in bt in the cost- so 4R of resources generates value, depending on inflation, to just under 6R, if generous 5 1/2R but likely more
2) 6R is p expensive
[2018-05-05 10:03] itsotter: two weeks ago i would not have considered that an unclickable smorcus was a better buy than tarsier
[2018-05-05 10:03] antlion7656: Seems I should leave Tarsier alone then
